title: Ghost mitigation for reflective lightguide
abstract: A reflective lightguide mitigates artifacts by overlapping a ghost image with a primary image. In some embodiments, the lightguide overlaps the ghost image and the primary image by matching an angle of the primary surface of each of the reflective facets of a reflective structure of the reflective lightguide to an angle of the secondary surface with respect to a horizontal plane of the reflective lightguide. Additionally, coating the secondary surface with a partially reflective coating further increases the amount of display light in the ghost image that is overlapped with the primary image.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method, comprising: directing display light through a reflective lightguide to form a primary image; directing the display light through the reflective lightguide to form a secondary image; and aligning the primary image and the secondary image. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein aligning the primary image and the secondary image comprises: reflecting display light forming the primary image from a first face of a reflective structure, the first face inclined at a first angle with respect to normal; and reflecting display light forming the secondary image from a second face of the reflective structure, the second face inclined at a second angle with respect to normal, wherein the first angle is approximately equal to the second angle. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the first angle differs from the second angle by less than approximately 4 x 10' 3 degrees. 4. The method of claim 2, wherein the first angle and the second angle are set to an angle with a range of approximately 20-89 degrees. 5. The method of any of claims 2 to 4, wherein the first face and the second face are coated with a partially reflective coating. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the partially reflective coating comprises a multilayer dielectric stack. 7. The method of any of claims 2 to 6, wherein the reflective structure forms at least one of an exit pupil expander or an outcoupler of the reflective lightguide. 8. A reflective lightguide, comprising: a reflective structure configured to direct display light through the reflective lightguide to form a primary image and a secondary image, wherein the primary image and the secondary image are aligned. 9. The reflective lightguide of claim 8, wherein the reflective structure comprises: a first face inclined at a first angle with respect to normal to reflect display light to form the primary image; and a second face inclined at a second angle with respect to normal to reflect display light to form the secondary image, wherein the first angle is approximately equal to the second angle. 10. The reflective lightguide of claim 9, wherein the first angle differs from the second angle by less than approximately 4 x 10 -3 degrees. 11 . The reflective lightguide of claim 9 or claim 10, wherein the first angle and the second angle are set to an angle with a range of approximately 20-89 degrees. 12. The reflective lightguide of any of claims 9 to 11 , wherein the first face and the second face are coated with a partially reflective coating. 13. The reflective lightguide of claim 12, wherein the partially reflective coating comprises a multi-layer dielectric stack. 14. The reflective lightguide of any of claims 9 to 13, wherein the reflective structure forms at least one of an exit pupil expander or an outcoupler of the reflective lightguide. 15. A reflective lightguide, comprising: a grating comprising a series of facets, wherein each facet comprises: a primary surface inclined at a first angle with respect to normal to reflect display light to form a primary image; and 

 a secondary surface inclined at a second angle with respect to normal to reflect display light to form a secondary image, wherein the first angle is approximately equal to the second angle. 16. The reflective lightguide of claim 15, wherein the first angle differs from the second angle by less than approximately 4 x 10' 3 degrees. 17. The reflective lightguide of claim 15 or 16, wherein the first angle and the second angle are set to an angle with a range of approximately 20-89 degrees. 18. The reflective lightguide of any of claims 15 to 17, wherein the primary surface and the secondary surface are coated with a partially reflective coating. 19. The reflective lightguide of claim 18, wherein the partially reflective coating comprises a multi-layer dielectric stack. 20. The reflective lightguide of any of claims 15 to 19, wherein the grating forms at least one of an exit pupil expander or an outcoupler of the reflective lightguide.
